assessment.title: Truck-Mount Operator Course (Module 03) — Practical Competency Assessment & Trainer Sign-Off
assessment.instructions:
## Practical competency assessment (trainer sign-off)

This is the legal competency record for a petrol-engine truck-mount (carpet-cleaning) operator. It is a manufacturer/operator competency assessment, not an IICRC CEC-accredited course. Sign-off certifies that the operator can run **the specific machine in front of them** safely and unsupervised; it does not certify regulatory approval of the vehicle or its installation.

### The two-gate structure

This assessment has two gates and **both must be completed for the same physical machine**:

- **Gate A — Trainer pre-flight (BEFORE delivery).** The trainer must verify every model- and engine-specific safety-critical setpoint (safe water/solution temperature, maximum working pressure, engine/pump/blower oil grades and service intervals, RPM, torque) against the **actual unit and engine manual**, plus the operator's State requirements (load-restraint guide, petrol 'tools of trade' limit, trade-waste disposal point, and the current Safe Work Australia CO exposure standard of 30 ppm 8-hour TWA reducing to 20 ppm in 2026, with a 35 ppm alarm setpoint). The course must not be delivered as fact, and no operator may be signed off, until every Gate A item is confirmed for this unit. This assessment reference does not substitute for the manufacturer's manual.
- **Gate B — Operator competency assessment.** The operator demonstrates each competency below on the actual machine. The trainer scores each rubric criterion against the observed demonstration.

### Evidence the operator MUST record (the legal record)

In the submission evidence the operator must record, as the legal record of exactly which machine they were signed off on:

- the **machine make**,
- the **model**,
- the **serial number** (read from the spec/serial plate), and
- the **trainer's (assessor's) name**.

A submission missing any of these four items is incomplete and cannot be accepted as a competency record.

### Zero-tolerance rule

Carbon monoxide is odourless, colourless and lethal within minutes. **Any failure on the carbon-monoxide control criterion is an automatic fail of the whole assessment, regardless of the total score achieved.** A pass therefore requires both the overall threshold to be met AND full competence on CO controls.

### Scoring

Score each criterion against what was actually observed on the machine. The pass threshold is 80%. Australian English, WHS terminology and metric units apply throughout.

criteria.label: Gate A — trainer manual verification (pre-flight)
criteria.description: Trainer confirms, and the record shows, that every model- and engine-specific setpoint (safe water/solution temperature, maximum working pressure, RPM, oil grades and service intervals) has been verified against the actual unit and engine manual, and that State-specific items (load restraint, petrol limit, trade-waste point, current CO exposure standard) are confirmed, before the assessment. No figure is assumed; the source is attached.

criteria.label: Pre-start walk-around
criteria.description: Performs the full fixed pre-start / daily walk-around: engine oil to mark (never overfilled), HP pump oil, blower oil (both sights, not overfilled), fuel adequate for the job and return trip, water supply and inlet hose flushed of debris, HP solution hose rated at or above 3,000 psi and 121 degrees C inspected for damage, vacuum hoses and inlet strainer clean, belts and safety guards fitted, chemical charged, and CO monitors and exhaust routing confirmed working.

criteria.label: Startup sequence in correct order
criteria.description: Positions the vehicle for exhaust safety first, then executes startup in the correct order — water supply on and water box filled first, then engine, then pressure — never dry-running the pump. If pressure does not build within about 5 seconds, stops and checks the water supply rather than persisting. Primes the chemical system to no air bubbles, warms up, and sets pressure and temperature for the tool and fibre without bypassing the low-pressure shutdown.

criteria.label: Shutdown and cool-down sequence in correct order
criteria.description: Executes shutdown in the correct order: flush the chemical system, clear and disconnect vacuum hoses, throttle to idle with thermostat lowest, run a cool-down of at least 5 minutes with the bypass valve open, end-of-day blower lube, ignition off, water off and supply bled, relieve residual pressure, then drain the waste tank at a proper disposal point. Observes the 20-minute hot-exhaust hold-off and states freeze-protection where relevant.

criteria.label: Pressure relief before opening any fitting
criteria.description: Demonstrates that before removing any cap, valve, plug, fitting or filter, the operator shuts down, cools, and relieves ALL pressure by triggering every tool hand-valve — the control against hot-water and high-pressure burns, which are the primary injury hazard. Never services a running machine and never points the hand-valve at any person.

criteria.label: Carbon monoxide controls — ZERO-TOLERANCE (automatic fail if not fully competent)
criteria.description: States that CO is odourless, colourless and lethal within minutes. Parks the vehicle outdoors with exhaust aimed away from every door, window, vent and air intake before starting (open doors/windows are not a substitute for outdoor parking), routes exhaust clear with a leak-proof clamp-on hose, fits and checks audible/visual CO monitors, and states the 35 ppm alarm rule (evacuate, shut the engine down, notify supervisor), the Australian exposure standard (30 ppm reducing to 20 ppm in 2026), the never-remain-in-the-van rule, and the never-enter-to-rescue-without-ventilating rule. Any shortfall here fails the whole assessment.

criteria.label: Load restraint, transport and storage
criteria.description: Secures the unit and tanks to the applicable load-restraint standard (0.8 g forward), states the GVM and axle check with full water and the registration/insurance modification trap, and stores fuel and chemicals lawfully — approved containers only, never in the cabin, with SDS carried in the vehicle. States lawful trade-waste disposal to an approved sewer point and never to stormwater, a waterway or the ground.

criteria.label: Maintenance and descaling awareness
criteria.description: Completes the daily maintenance walk-around, states the 5/50/100/500/1,000-hour service anchors, tests water hardness for the operator's supply and states when a softener and descale become mandatory, and diagnoses low heat, low pressure and loss of vacuum from their symptoms.

criteria.label: Emergency response
criteria.description: States and demonstrates the correct immediate response to a CO alarm or suspected exposure (evacuate to fresh air, shut the engine down, notify supervisor, call 000, do not re-enter unventilated to rescue), an HP hose burst (release hand-valve, shut down, cool, relieve all pressure before touching fittings), an engine low-oil-pressure shutdown (do not restart until the cause is fixed), a loss of pump pressure, and a petrol spill or fire (no ignition after a spill until cleaned up).
